Stroke Dataset

Building and evaluating a prediction model

Sivakamakshi Muthu Kumarasamy, ID = 25240080

25 March 2026

library(tidyverse)
library(table1)
library(dplyr)
library(plotly)
library(caret)
library(pROC)
library(randomForest)
library(gbm)
library(PRROC)
library(dcurves)
library(patchwork)
library(kableExtra)
library(scales)
library(MASS)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
library(e1071)
library(tree)
library(BART)
library(ROSE)

Prediction Goal

Dataset

Dataset: Stroke Prediction Dataset

Source: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/fedesoriano/stroke-prediction-dataset?resource=download

The study design is cross-sectional with adult patients with demographic, clinical and lifestyle characteristics.

Dataset and Pre-processing

set.seed(123)
stroke <- read.csv("stroke dataset.csv",
                   stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
                   na.strings = c("NA", "N/A", "", " "))

stroke <- stroke %>%
  filter(gender != "Other") %>%
  dplyr::select(-id) %>%
  mutate(
    bmi            = as.numeric(bmi),
   bmi = ave(bmi, stroke, FUN = function(x) ifelse(is.na(x), median(x, na.rm=TRUE), x)),
    stroke         = factor(stroke, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    gender         = factor(gender),
    ever_married   = factor(ever_married),
    work_type      = factor(work_type),
    Residence_type = factor(Residence_type),
    smoking_status = factor(smoking_status),
    hypertension   = factor(hypertension),
    heart_disease  = factor(heart_disease)
  )
names(stroke)
##  [1] "gender"            "age"               "hypertension"     
##  [4] "heart_disease"     "ever_married"      "work_type"        
##  [7] "Residence_type"    "avg_glucose_level" "bmi"              
## [10] "smoking_status"    "stroke"
summary(stroke)
##     gender          age        hypertension heart_disease ever_married
##  Female:2994   Min.   : 0.08   0:4611       0:4833        No :1756    
##  Male  :2115   1st Qu.:25.00   1: 498       1: 276        Yes:3353    
##                Median :45.00                                          
##                Mean   :43.23                                          
##                3rd Qu.:61.00                                          
##                Max.   :82.00                                          
##          work_type    Residence_type avg_glucose_level      bmi       
##  children     : 687   Rural:2513     Min.   : 55.12    Min.   :10.30  
##  Govt_job     : 657   Urban:2596     1st Qu.: 77.24    1st Qu.:23.80  
##  Never_worked :  22                  Median : 91.88    Median :28.00  
##  Private      :2924                  Mean   :106.14    Mean   :28.87  
##  Self-employed: 819                  3rd Qu.:114.09    3rd Qu.:32.80  
##                                      Max.   :271.74    Max.   :97.60  
##          smoking_status stroke    
##  formerly smoked: 884   No :4860  
##  never smoked   :1892   Yes: 249  
##  smokes         : 789             
##  Unknown        :1544             
##                                   
## 
colSums(is.na(stroke))
##            gender               age      hypertension     heart_disease 
##                 0                 0                 0                 0 
##      ever_married         work_type    Residence_type avg_glucose_level 
##                 0                 0                 0                 0 
##               bmi    smoking_status            stroke 
##                 0                 0                 0
n_stroke <- sum(stroke$stroke == "Yes")
n_stroke
## [1] 249
male <- sum(stroke$gender == "Male")
female <- sum(stroke$gender == "Female")

male
## [1] 2115
female
## [1] 2994

Exploratory Visualisation

#Age distribution by Stroke status

age_plot <- ggplot(stroke, aes(x = age, fill = stroke)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, alpha = 0.75, position = "identity") +
  labs(title = "Age distribution by Stroke status", x = "Age", y = "Count", fill = "Stroke") + 
  theme_minimal()

# Stroke status by average glucose levels
glucose_plot <- ggplot(stroke, aes(x = avg_glucose_level, fill = stroke)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, alpha = 0.75, position = "identity") +
  labs(title = "Stroke status by average glucose levels", x = "Average glucose levels", y = "Count", fill = "Stroke") + 
  theme_minimal()


# Stroke rate by BMI category
bmi_plot <- ggplot(stroke, aes(x = stroke, y = bmi, fill  = stroke)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Stroke status by BMI levels", x = "Stroke", y = "BMI", fill = "Stroke") +
  theme_minimal()

#Stroke rate by heart disease

heart_plot <- stroke %>%
  count(heart_disease, stroke) %>%
  group_by(heart_disease) %>%
  mutate(proportion = n / sum(n)) %>%
  filter(stroke == "Yes") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = heart_disease, y = proportion, fill = heart_disease)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format())+
  labs(title  = "Stroke status by heart diseases", x = "Heart Disease  0 = No, 1 = Yes", y = "Stroke", fill = "Stroke") +
  theme_minimal()

#Stroke status by hypertension
  
htn_plot <- stroke%>%
  count(hypertension, stroke) %>%
  group_by(hypertension) %>%
  mutate(proportion = n / sum(n)) %>%
  filter(stroke == "Yes") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = hypertension, y = proportion, fill = hypertension)) +
  geom_col()+
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format())+
  labs(title = "Stroke status by hypertension", x = "Hypertension 0 = No, 1 = Yes", y = "Stroke", fill = "Stroke")+
  theme_minimal()

(age_plot | glucose_plot) /  bmi_plot / (heart_plot | htn_plot)

Cross-validation

# Train/test split

set.seed(123)
n           <- nrow(stroke)
train_index <- sample(1:n, size = 0.7 * n)
train       <- stroke[train_index, ]
test        <- stroke[-train_index, ]

cat("Training set size:", nrow(train), "\n")
## Training set size: 3576
cat("Test set size:",     nrow(test),  "\n")
## Test set size: 1533
test_actual <- factor(test$stroke, levels = c("No", "Yes"))

cat("Training set stroke prevalence:", round(mean(train$stroke == "Yes")*100, 2), "%\n")
## Training set stroke prevalence: 4.73 %
cat("Test set stroke prevalence:", round(mean(test$stroke == "Yes")*100, 2), "%\n")
## Test set stroke prevalence: 5.22 %
set.seed(123)

train_bal <- ovun.sample(stroke ~ ., data = train, method = "both")$data

cat("Balanced training set stroke prevalence:",
    round(mean(train_bal$stroke == "Yes") * 100, 2), "%\n")
## Balanced training set stroke prevalence: 49.72 %

Logistic Regression - Full Model

log_model <- glm(stroke ~., data = train_bal, family = binomial)

summary(log_model)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = stroke ~ ., family = binomial, data = train_bal)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                              Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                 -3.831306   0.357166 -10.727  < 2e-16 ***
## genderMale                  -0.252271   0.089280  -2.826 0.004719 ** 
## age                          0.074270   0.003292  22.560  < 2e-16 ***
## hypertension1                0.689550   0.113626   6.069 1.29e-09 ***
## heart_disease1               0.246538   0.142137   1.735 0.082827 .  
## ever_marriedYes              0.140180   0.139147   1.007 0.313732    
## work_typeGovt_job           -1.292943   0.383011  -3.376 0.000736 ***
## work_typeNever_worked      -12.110872 277.764254  -0.044 0.965222    
## work_typePrivate            -0.963581   0.369959  -2.605 0.009199 ** 
## work_typeSelf-employed      -1.539002   0.392485  -3.921 8.81e-05 ***
## Residence_typeUrban          0.004743   0.084638   0.056 0.955315    
## avg_glucose_level            0.004633   0.000846   5.476 4.35e-08 ***
## bmi                          0.004947   0.006846   0.723 0.469934    
## smoking_statusnever smoked  -0.322809   0.109131  -2.958 0.003097 ** 
## smoking_statussmokes         0.021749   0.133594   0.163 0.870678    
## smoking_statusUnknown       -0.264811   0.133562  -1.983 0.047403 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 4957.3  on 3575  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 3430.6  on 3560  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 3462.6
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 13
# Prediction

log_pred <- predict(log_model, newdata = test, type = "response")

roc_curve <- roc(test$stroke, log_pred)
## Setting levels: control = No, case = Yes
## Setting direction: controls < cases
best_thresh_log <- coords(roc_curve, "best", ret = "threshold")$threshold

log_pred_class <- factor(ifelse(log_pred > best_thresh_log, "Yes", "No"),
                          levels = c("No", "Yes"))


confusionMatrix(log_pred_class, test_actual, positive = "Yes")
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction  No Yes
##        No  985  10
##        Yes 468  70
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.6882          
##                  95% CI : (0.6643, 0.7113)
##     No Information Rate : 0.9478          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 1               
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.1492          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : <2e-16          
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.87500         
##             Specificity : 0.67791         
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.13011         
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.98995         
##              Prevalence : 0.05219         
##          Detection Rate : 0.04566         
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.35095         
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.77645         
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : Yes             
## 
plot(roc_curve, col = "steelblue", main = "ROC Curve - Logistic Regression")

auc_full <- auc(roc_curve)

cat("AUC (logistic):", round(auc_full, 3), "\n")
## AUC (logistic): 0.842
# Variable Selection

step_model <- stepAIC(log_model, direction = "both",trace = FALSE)
summary(step_model)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = stroke ~ gender + age + hypertension + heart_disease + 
##     work_type + avg_glucose_level + smoking_status, family = binomial, 
##     data = train_bal)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                              Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                -3.739e+00  3.329e-01 -11.233  < 2e-16 ***
## genderMale                 -2.497e-01  8.924e-02  -2.798 0.005141 ** 
## age                         7.446e-02  3.152e-03  23.625  < 2e-16 ***
## hypertension1               6.880e-01  1.132e-01   6.075 1.24e-09 ***
## heart_disease1              2.312e-01  1.416e-01   1.634 0.102357    
## work_typeGovt_job          -1.147e+00  3.639e-01  -3.151 0.001626 ** 
## work_typeNever_worked      -1.206e+01  2.779e+02  -0.043 0.965378    
## work_typePrivate           -8.092e-01  3.477e-01  -2.327 0.019943 *  
## work_typeSelf-employed     -1.386e+00  3.717e-01  -3.729 0.000193 ***
## avg_glucose_level           4.887e-03  7.956e-04   6.143 8.11e-10 ***
## smoking_statusnever smoked -3.351e-01  1.087e-01  -3.084 0.002041 ** 
## smoking_statussmokes        2.012e-02  1.334e-01   0.151 0.880080    
## smoking_statusUnknown      -2.776e-01  1.330e-01  -2.088 0.036785 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 4957.3  on 3575  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 3432.3  on 3563  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 3458.3
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 13
AIC(log_model, step_model)
##            df      AIC
## log_model  16 3462.586
## step_model 13 3458.256
# Predictions

step_pred <- predict(step_model, newdata = test, type = "response")

roc_step <- roc(test$stroke, step_pred)
## Setting levels: control = No, case = Yes
## Setting direction: controls < cases
best_step_thresh <- coords(roc_step, "best", ret="threshold")$threshold

step_pred_class <- factor(ifelse(step_pred > 0.5, "Yes", "No"), levels = c("No", "Yes"))

# Confusion Matrix
confusionMatrix(step_pred_class, test_actual, positive = "Yes")
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction   No  Yes
##        No  1096   21
##        Yes  357   59
##                                          
##                Accuracy : 0.7534         
##                  95% CI : (0.731, 0.7748)
##     No Information Rate : 0.9478         
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 1              
##                                          
##                   Kappa : 0.1648         
##                                          
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : <2e-16         
##                                          
##             Sensitivity : 0.73750        
##             Specificity : 0.75430        
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.14183        
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.98120        
##              Prevalence : 0.05219        
##          Detection Rate : 0.03849        
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.27136        
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.74590        
##                                          
##        'Positive' Class : Yes            
## 
# ROC and AUC

plot(roc_step, col="blue", main="ROC Curve - Stepwise Logistic Regression")

auc_step <- auc(roc_step)
cat("AUC (stepwise):", round(auc_step, 3), "\n")
## AUC (stepwise): 0.843
# Variable Importance

step_varimp <- varImp(step_model, scale = TRUE)
step_varimp_df <- as.data.frame(step_varimp)
step_varimp_df$Variable <- rownames(step_varimp_df)
rownames(step_varimp_df) <- NULL

imp_col <- names(step_varimp_df)[1]
step_varimp_df <- step_varimp_df %>% arrange(desc(.data[[imp_col]]))

step_varimp_df %>% kable(caption = "Variable Importance - Stepwise Logistic Regression")
Variable Importance - Stepwise Logistic Regression
Overall Variable
23.6245183 age
6.1427262 avg_glucose_level
6.0753373 hypertension1
3.7285372 work_typeSelf-employed
3.1512227 work_typeGovt_job
3.0841692 smoking_statusnever smoked
2.7980434 genderMale
2.3274092 work_typePrivate
2.0881385 smoking_statusUnknown
1.6335346 heart_disease1
0.1508677 smoking_statussmokes
0.0434053 work_typeNever_worked 
ggplot(step_varimp_df, aes(x = reorder(Variable, .data[[imp_col]]), y = .data[[imp_col]])) +
  geom_col(fill = "green") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Variable Importance - Stepwise Logistic Regression",
       x = "Variable",
       y = "Importance (scaled)") +
  theme_minimal()

# Decision Tree

set.seed(123)
tree_model <- rpart(
  stroke ~ .,
  data    = train_bal,
  method  = "class",
  control = rpart.control(cp = 0.01, minsplit = 20, minbucket = 7)
)
printcp(tree_model)
## 
## Classification tree:
## rpart(formula = stroke ~ ., data = train_bal, method = "class", 
##     control = rpart.control(cp = 0.01, minsplit = 20, minbucket = 7))
## 
## Variables actually used in tree construction:
## [1] age               avg_glucose_level hypertension      smoking_status   
## 
## Root node error: 1778/3576 = 0.4972
## 
## n= 3576 
## 
##        CP nsplit rel error  xerror     xstd
## 1 0.50450      0   1.00000 1.02868 0.016812
## 2 0.01706      1   0.49550 0.50506 0.014585
## 3 0.01000      6   0.40439 0.43926 0.013896
plotcp(tree_model)

cp_table <- tree_model$cptable
min_idx   <- which.min(cp_table[,"xerror"])
threshold <- cp_table[min_idx, "xerror"] + cp_table[min_idx, "xstd"]
best_cp   <- cp_table[min(which(cp_table[,"xerror"] <= threshold)), "CP"]
tree_pruned <- prune(tree_model, cp = best_cp)

rpart.plot(tree_pruned, main = "Pruned decision tree")

tree_prob <- predict(tree_pruned, test, type = "prob")[, "Yes"]

tree_class <- factor(ifelse(tree_prob > 0.5, "Yes", "No"), levels = c("No", "Yes"))

confusionMatrix(tree_class, test_actual, positive = "Yes")
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction   No  Yes
##        No  1081   16
##        Yes  372   64
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.7469          
##                  95% CI : (0.7244, 0.7685)
##     No Information Rate : 0.9478          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 1               
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.1753          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : <2e-16          
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.80000         
##             Specificity : 0.74398         
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.14679         
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.98541         
##              Prevalence : 0.05219         
##          Detection Rate : 0.04175         
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.28441         
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.77199         
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : Yes             
## 
roc_tree <- roc(test$stroke, tree_prob)
## Setting levels: control = No, case = Yes
## Setting direction: controls < cases
best_thresh_tree <- coords(roc_tree, "best", ret = "threshold")$threshold

auc_tree <- auc(roc_tree)
cat("AUC (tree):",     round(auc_tree, 3), "\n")
## AUC (tree): 0.819
plot(roc_tree, col = "darkorange", main = "ROC – Decision Tree")

# Random Forest

set.seed(123)
rf_model <- randomForest(
  stroke ~ .,
  data       = train_bal,
  ntree      = 200,
  importance = TRUE
)

print(rf_model)
## 
## Call:
##  randomForest(formula = stroke ~ ., data = train_bal, ntree = 200,      importance = TRUE) 
##                Type of random forest: classification
##                      Number of trees: 200
## No. of variables tried at each split: 3
## 
##         OOB estimate of  error rate: 1.37%
## Confusion matrix:
##       No  Yes  class.error
## No  1750   48 0.0266963293
## Yes    1 1777 0.0005624297
# Predictions
rf_pred <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test)
rf_prob <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test, type = "prob")[,2]

# Confusion Matrix
confusionMatrix(rf_pred, test_actual, positive = "Yes")
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction   No  Yes
##        No  1394   60
##        Yes   59   20
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.9224          
##                  95% CI : (0.9078, 0.9353)
##     No Information Rate : 0.9478          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 1               
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.2106          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 1               
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.25000         
##             Specificity : 0.95939         
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.25316         
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.95873         
##              Prevalence : 0.05219         
##          Detection Rate : 0.01305         
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.05153         
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.60470         
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : Yes             
## 
# ROC and AUC
roc_rf <- roc(test$stroke, rf_prob)
## Setting levels: control = No, case = Yes
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_rf, col="darkgreen", main="ROC Curve - Random Forest")

auc_rf <- auc(roc_rf)
cat("AUC for Random Forest:", round(auc_rf,3), "\n")
## AUC for Random Forest: 0.838
varImpPlot(rf_model, main="Variable Importance - Random Forest")

model_summary <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Logistic Full", "Logistic Stepwise", "Decision Tree", "Random Forest"),
  AUC = c(auc_full, auc_step, auc_tree, auc_rf)
)
print(model_summary)
##               Model       AUC
## 1     Logistic Full 0.8424983
## 2 Logistic Stepwise 0.8425843
## 3     Decision Tree 0.8189909
## 4     Random Forest 0.8379990

#Complete Tree Analysis

table(stroke$stroke)
## 
##   No  Yes 
## 4860  249
set.seed(123)
tree.stroke.train <- tree(stroke ~ ., data = train_bal)
print("Tree Summary")
## [1] "Tree Summary"
summary(tree.stroke.train)
## 
## Classification tree:
## tree(formula = stroke ~ ., data = train_bal)
## Variables actually used in tree construction:
## [1] "age"               "bmi"               "avg_glucose_level"
## Number of terminal nodes:  13 
## Residual mean deviance:  0.8126 = 2895 / 3563 
## Misclassification error rate: 0.2109 = 754 / 3576
plot(tree.stroke.train)
text(tree.stroke.train, pretty = 0)

#Predictions

tree.pred <- predict(tree.stroke.train, test, type = "class")
table(tree.pred, test$stroke)
##          
## tree.pred   No  Yes
##       No  1176   28
##       Yes  277   52
mean(tree.pred != test$stroke)
## [1] 0.1989563
#CV pruning

set.seed(123)
cv.stroke <- cv.tree(tree.stroke.train, FUN = prune.misclass)

names(cv.stroke)
## [1] "size"   "dev"    "k"      "method"
par(mfrow = c(1,2))
plot(cv.stroke$size, cv.stroke$dev, type = "b",
     xlab = "Tree Size", ylab = "Deviance")

plot(cv.stroke$k, cv.stroke$dev, type = "b",
     xlab = "Complexity Parameter", ylab = "Deviance")

par(mfrow = c(1,1))
best.size <- cv.stroke$size[which.min(cv.stroke$dev)]

prune.stroke <- prune.misclass(tree.stroke.train, best = best.size)
plot(prune.stroke)
text(prune.stroke, pretty = 0)

#Testing pruned tree

pruned.pred <- predict(prune.stroke, test, type = "class")
table(pruned.pred, test$stroke)
##            
## pruned.pred   No  Yes
##         No  1176   28
##         Yes  277   52
mean(pruned.pred != test$stroke)
## [1] 0.1989563
p_vars <- ncol(train_bal) - 1

set.seed(1)
bag_model <- randomForest(stroke ~ ., data = train_bal,
                          mtry = p_vars, importance = TRUE)
bag_model
## 
## Call:
##  randomForest(formula = stroke ~ ., data = train_bal, mtry = p_vars,      importance = TRUE) 
##                Type of random forest: classification
##                      Number of trees: 500
## No. of variables tried at each split: 10
## 
##         OOB estimate of  error rate: 1.59%
## Confusion matrix:
##       No  Yes  class.error
## No  1742   56 0.0311457175
## Yes    1 1777 0.0005624297
# Predictions
bag_pred <- predict(bag_model, newdata = test)
bag_prob <- predict(bag_model, newdata = test, type = "prob")[, 2]

# Confusion Matrix
confusionMatrix(bag_pred, test_actual, positive = "Yes")
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction   No  Yes
##        No  1373   60
##        Yes   80   20
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.9087          
##                  95% CI : (0.8931, 0.9226)
##     No Information Rate : 0.9478          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 1.0000          
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.1743          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.1083          
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.25000         
##             Specificity : 0.94494         
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.20000         
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.95813         
##              Prevalence : 0.05219         
##          Detection Rate : 0.01305         
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.06523         
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.59747         
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : Yes             
## 
# ROC and AUC
roc_bag <- roc(ifelse(test_actual == "Yes", 1, 0), bag_prob)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_bag, col = "hotpink", main = "ROC Curve - Bagging")

auc_bag <- auc(roc_bag)
cat("AUC for Bagging:", round(auc_bag, 3), "\n")
## AUC for Bagging: 0.813
varImpPlot(bag_model, main = "Variable Importance - Bagging")

train_gbm        <- train_bal
train_gbm$stroke <- ifelse(train_gbm$stroke == "Yes", 1, 0)

set.seed(1)
boost_model <- gbm(stroke ~ ., data = train_gbm,
                   distribution = "bernoulli",
                   n.trees = 5000,
                   interaction.depth = 4,
                   shrinkage = 0.001,
                   cv.folds = 10,
                   verbose = FALSE)

best_iter <- gbm.perf(boost_model, method = "cv")

cat("Best number of trees (CV):", best_iter, "\n")
## Best number of trees (CV): 5000
summary(boost_model)

##                                 var     rel.inf
## age                             age 66.02158839
## bmi                             bmi 16.13593521
## avg_glucose_level avg_glucose_level 11.34220555
## hypertension           hypertension  2.72916186
## smoking_status       smoking_status  1.38589211
## work_type                 work_type  1.25329350
## heart_disease         heart_disease  0.78119922
## gender                       gender  0.16674681
## Residence_type       Residence_type  0.12948188
## ever_married           ever_married  0.05449546
# Partial dependence plots for top 2 predictors
top2 <- summary(boost_model, plotit = FALSE)$var[1:2]
plot(boost_model, i = top2[1])

plot(boost_model, i = top2[2])

# Predictions
boost_prob  <- predict(boost_model, newdata = test,
                       n.trees = best_iter, type = "response")
boost_class <- factor(ifelse(boost_prob > 0.5, "Yes", "No"),
                      levels = c("No", "Yes"))

# Confusion Matrix
confusionMatrix(boost_class, test_actual, positive = "Yes")
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction   No  Yes
##        No  1137   18
##        Yes  316   62
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.7821          
##                  95% CI : (0.7606, 0.8026)
##     No Information Rate : 0.9478          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 1               
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.202           
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : <2e-16          
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.77500         
##             Specificity : 0.78252         
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.16402         
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.98442         
##              Prevalence : 0.05219         
##          Detection Rate : 0.04044         
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.24658         
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.77876         
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : Yes             
## 
# ROC and AUC
roc_boost <- roc(ifelse(test_actual == "Yes", 1, 0), boost_prob)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_boost, col = "purple", main = "ROC Curve - Boosting")

cat("AUC for Boosting:", round(auc(roc_boost), 3), "\n")
## AUC for Boosting: 0.854
# BART

x_train <- model.matrix(stroke ~ . - 1, data = train)
y_train <- as.numeric(train$stroke == "Yes")
x_test  <- model.matrix(stroke ~ . - 1, data = test)

set.seed(1)
bartfit <- pbart(x.train = x_train,
                 y.train = y_train,
                 x.test  = x_test)
## *****Into main of pbart
## *****Data:
## data:n,p,np: 3576, 16, 1533
## y1,yn: 0, 0
## x1,x[n*p]: 1.000000, 0.000000
## xp1,xp[np*p]: 1.000000, 1.000000
## *****Number of Trees: 50
## *****Number of Cut Points: 1 ... 1
## *****burn and ndpost: 100, 1000
## *****Prior:mybeta,alpha,tau: 2.000000,0.950000,0.212132
## *****binaryOffset: -1.672027
## *****Dirichlet:sparse,theta,omega,a,b,rho,augment: 0,0,1,0.5,1,16,0
## *****nkeeptrain,nkeeptest,nkeeptreedraws: 1000,1000,1000
## *****printevery: 100
## *****skiptr,skipte,skiptreedraws: 1,1,1
## 
## MCMC
## done 0 (out of 1100)
## done 100 (out of 1100)
## done 200 (out of 1100)
## done 300 (out of 1100)
## done 400 (out of 1100)
## done 500 (out of 1100)
## done 600 (out of 1100)
## done 700 (out of 1100)
## done 800 (out of 1100)
## done 900 (out of 1100)
## done 1000 (out of 1100)
## time: 9s
## check counts
## trcnt,tecnt: 1000,1000
# Posterior mean predicted probabilities
bart_prob  <- bartfit$prob.test.mean
bart_class <- factor(ifelse(bart_prob > 0.5, "Yes", "No"),
                     levels = c("No", "Yes"))

# Confusion Matrix
confusionMatrix(bart_class, test_actual, positive = "Yes")
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction   No  Yes
##        No  1453   79
##        Yes    0    1
##                                          
##                Accuracy : 0.9485         
##                  95% CI : (0.9362, 0.959)
##     No Information Rate : 0.9478         
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.4839         
##                                          
##                   Kappa : 0.0234         
##                                          
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : <2e-16         
##                                          
##             Sensitivity : 0.0125000      
##             Specificity : 1.0000000      
##          Pos Pred Value : 1.0000000      
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.9484334      
##              Prevalence : 0.0521853      
##          Detection Rate : 0.0006523      
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.0006523      
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.5062500      
##                                          
##        'Positive' Class : Yes            
## 
# ROC and AUC
roc_bart <- roc(ifelse(test_actual == "Yes", 1, 0), bart_prob)
## Setting levels: control = 0, case = 1
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_bart, col = "brown", main = "ROC Curve - BART")

cat("AUC for BART:", round(auc(roc_bart), 3), "\n")
## AUC for BART: 0.848
# Variable inclusion counts (equivalent of variable importance)
ord <- order(bartfit$varcount.mean, decreasing = TRUE)
bartfit$varcount.mean[ord]
##                        age     work_typeSelf-employed 
##                      7.834                      4.881 
##               genderFemale          avg_glucose_level 
##                      4.193                      3.934 
##       smoking_statussmokes                 genderMale 
##                      3.914                      3.783 
##             heart_disease1      work_typeNever_worked 
##                      3.546                      3.375 
##           work_typePrivate          work_typeGovt_job 
##                      3.314                      3.312 
##              hypertension1      smoking_statusUnknown 
##                      3.278                      3.193 
##                        bmi        Residence_typeUrban 
##                      3.050                      3.027 
## smoking_statusnever smoked            ever_marriedYes 
##                      2.900                      2.496
# ROC curves for all models
plot(roc_curve,  col = "steelblue",   lwd = 2,
     main = "ROC comparison - all models")
plot(roc_step,   col = "dodgerblue",  lwd = 2, lty = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(roc_tree,   col = "darkorange",  lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(roc_bag,    col = "hotpink",     lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(roc_rf,     col = "forestgreen", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(roc_boost,  col = "purple",      lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(roc_bart,   col = "brown",       lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 3, col = "grey60")

legend("bottomright", bty = "n",
       legend = c(
         paste("Logistic (full)     AUC =", round(auc(roc_curve), 3)),
         paste("Logistic (stepwise) AUC =", round(auc(roc_step),  3)),
         paste("Decision tree       AUC =", round(auc(roc_tree),  3)),
         paste("Bagging             AUC =", round(auc_bag,        3)),
         paste("Random forest       AUC =", round(auc_rf,         3)),
         paste("Boosting            AUC =", round(auc(roc_boost), 3)),
         paste("BART                AUC =", round(auc(roc_bart),  3))
       ),
       col = c("steelblue", "dodgerblue", "darkorange",
               "hotpink", "forestgreen", "purple", "brown"),
       lwd = 2)
